title: thymus serpyllum var. ericoides
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. ericoides, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb belonging to the mint family. This plant is known for its small, pink or white flowers and its fragrance, which is often used in herbal remedies. Creeping thyme has been traditionally used for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing stress, promoting digestion, and relieving respiratory issues. Creeping thyme can be prepared as a tea, oil, or tincture, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
